era_context: Mid-second millennium BCE: New Kingdom Egypt consolidating power, Hittite Empire expanding, Shang Dynasty in China, Mycenaean Greece in the early palace period. This is an era of palace-centred states, chariot warfare, and emerging long-distance trade networks.

events.title: Ahmose I's reign: Egyptian consolidation and military campaigns
events.description: Ahmose I continues his reign, consolidating Egyptian power and conducting military campaigns to secure Egypt's borders and establish hegemony in Nubia and the Levant. This period is part of the broader New Kingdom consolidation that follows the expulsion of the Hyksos.
events.certainty_note: Ahmose I's reign is well-documented, but specific events are not precisely dated to individual years.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are definitively documented for 1552 BCE. The year falls within Ahmose I's reign, but no contemporary source explicitly dates events to this year.
historiographic_note: The year 1552 BCE is poorly documented. No specific events are definitively assigned to this year in surviving sources. The historical record for this year is essentially a continuation of broader trends from adjacent years.
